title: Retrospective Review Protocol and Methodological Transparency Statement: Floating and Amphibious Architecture in Waterfront Built Environments
author: Gorzka, Jakub
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This document provides a retrospective review protocol and methodological transparency statement prepared during the revision of the manuscript Floating and Amphibious Architecture in Waterfront Built Environments: A Systematic Review of Climate Adaptation and Regenerative Potential (sustainability-4314880). The protocol documents the review aim, research questions, search strategy, eligibility criteria, study-selection procedure, data-extraction fields, coding framework, operationalisation of regenerative potential, quality-control procedures, evidence-strength appraisal and synthesis approach. The review was not prospectively registered before the search and screening process and does not constitute a preregistration. It distinguishes between the screened publication landscape used for bibliometric profiling and the core evidence base used for qualitative synthesis. The protocol is intended to improve methodological transparency, clarify coding and appraisal decisions, and support assessment of the reproducibility of the review process.
